name: Easy Clam Sauce with Low-Calorie Version
recipeIngredient: 6 1/2 oz Can Minced Or Diced Clams
1 tbsp Oregano or Basil, chopped (or a mixture of both)
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the juice from the can of clams into a small saucepan and set aside the clams.
2. Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Add minced garlic and sauté until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
3. Stir in oregano or basil (or the mixture) along with salt and pepper.
4. Bring the garlic mixture to a simmer, then return the clam juice to the pot.
5. Cook the mixture gently until heated through.
6. Drain the pasta you plan to serve with the sauce and add it back into the pot.
7. Remove the pan from heat. Add the clams to the pasta and toss everything together.
8. Let the dish stand for a few minutes so the juices can permeate the pasta evenly before serving.
9. Sprinkle grated Parmesan or Romano cheese over the top, if desired (though not traditional).
10. Remember, canned clams should never be cooked as they will turn into hard shells.
